title: Anchovy Cheese Bread
excerpt: Ingredients 1 smallish French loaf 50 ml mayonnaise 15 ml Dijon mustard 250 g cheddar cheese 1 tin asparagus pieces, drained fresh basil, thyme and dill 10 anchovies (or as many or as few as you please) extra basil leaves for garnishing Method Cut the bread lengthways and hollow out the bottom …
html:
Ingredients

  • 1 smallish French loaf
  • 50 ml mayonnaise
  • 15 ml Dijon mustard
  • 250 g cheddar cheese
  • 1 tin asparagus pieces, drained
  • fresh basil, thyme and dill
  • 10 anchovies (or as many or as few as you please)
  • extra basil leaves for garnishing

Method

Cut the bread lengthways and hollow out the bottom half. Spread the inside of the bread with the mayonnaise and mustard.

Fill the hollowed-out side of the bread with plenty of cheese, arrange the asparagus pieces on top and sprinkle with the fresh herbs. Place the anchovies on top, place the top half of the bread on top of the cheese and wrap the bread in foil.

Bake in a preheated oven for 25 minutes at 180ºC. Remove the foil, slice and serve while still warm.

Serves about 4 with soup.

Easy!
